title: hieracium webbianum subsp. tarradasum
intro: Hieracium webbianum subsp. tarradasum, also known as the Meadow Hawkweed, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its bright yellow or orange flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to possess medicinal properties that can aid in wound healing and the treatment of various skin conditions. The health benefits of Hieracium webbianum subsp. tarradasum include its ability to reduce swelling and promote the healing of wounds, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol, and are often used to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties.
